swipes.file: Swipe – The Iran War's Energy Shock – 2026-07-24.html
swipes.title: The Iran War's Energy Shock
swipes.description: Ones and Tooze (Foreign Policy, Ep. 244, 29 May 2026): Adam Tooze on the deals that could end the 2026 Iran war — and the 33-km Strait of Hormuz that carries a fifth of the world's oil.

swipes.title: Nexus
swipes.description: Yuval Noah Harari (2024): a history of information networks from the Stone Age to AI — information's job is to connect, not to reveal truth, and we're now handing the network to a non-human intelligence.

swipes.title: Die Duineser Elegien
swipes.description: Lyrik · Rilkes 'Duineser Elegien' (1912–1922): zehn Elegien zwischen Klage und Rühmen. Am Schloss Duino im Sturm gehört, erst nach zehn Jahren in Muzot vollendet.

swipes.title: Ana Mendieta
swipes.description: Exhibition · Ana Mendieta at Tate Modern (15 Jul 2026 – 17 Jan 2027): the UK's biggest survey in over a decade of the artist who pressed her body into the earth — 120+ 'earth-body' works.

swipes.title: Zeit der Zauberer
swipes.description: Wolfram Eilenberger (2018): das große Jahrzehnt der Philosophie 1919–1929 in den Leben von Cassirer, Benjamin, Heidegger und Wittgenstein — gerahmt von der Davoser Disputation 1929 (Cassirer gegen Heidegger).

swipes.title: Triggerpunkte
swipes.description: Steffen Mau, Thomas Lux & Linus Westheuser (2023): Die vielbeschworene Polarisierung ist ein Mythos — in vier Ungleichheitsarenen herrscht breiter Konsens; Streit entzündet sich erst an konkreten Reizpunkten wie Gendersternchen und Lastenrädern.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Salt of the Earth – 2026-07-23.html
swipes.title: The Salt of the Earth
swipes.description: Photography · Sebastião Salgado (1944–2025): the Brazilian master of black-and-white documentary photography — Workers, Migrations, Genesis — honoured by posthumous retrospectives across 2026 in Paris and Bassano.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Man Who Solved the Market – 2026-07-23.html
swipes.title: The Man Who Solved the Market
swipes.description: Gregory Zuckerman (2019): how Jim Simons — a Chern–Simons geometer and Cold-War codebreaker who never read an earnings report — built Renaissance Technologies and the Medallion Fund (≈66% a year) into the greatest money-making machine in history.

swipes.title: The Fate of Rome
swipes.description: Kyle Harper (2017): the first history to put climate and disease at the centre of Rome's fall — volcanoes, solar cycles and three great pandemics (Antonine, Cyprian, Justinian) as the empire's unseen adversaries. 'Nature is not passive.'

swipes.title: Kind of Blue
swipes.description: Music · Miles Davis (1959): the best-selling jazz record of all time, built on modal improvisation instead of chord changes — 'a new language of music' whose influence runs from the Doors to Steve Reich; freshly remastered in 2026.

swipes.file: Swipe – Julius Caesar und der Rubikon – 2026-07-23.html
swipes.title: Julius Caesar und der Rubikon
swipes.description: Eine Stunde History (Deutschlandfunk Nova, 17. Juli 2026): Gaius Julius Caesar — vom Gallischen Krieg über Alesia und den Rubikon ('Alea iacta est') bis zur Ermordung an den Iden des März 44 v. Chr.

swipes.file: Swipe – Hyperscaler Debt Is Now a Rates Story – 2026-07-23.html
swipes.title: Hyperscaler Debt Is Now a Rates Story
swipes.description: Odd Lots (Bloomberg, July 2026): the AI buildout is increasingly funded by bonds, not cash flow — Big Five capex hit ~$602B, AI debt doubled to ~$350B, and Amazon's $25B bond was the softest launch since 2025. Now it's a rates story.

swipes.title: Agile Governance
swipes.description: Sinica Podcast (Kaiser Kuo, 1 July 2026): Tsinghua's Xue Lan on how China regulates AI it can't fully predict — the 'pacing problem', 'agile governance', and whether Beijing's adaptive model can travel to democracies. Recorded at Summer Davos, Dalian.

swipes.title: Waiting for Godot
swipes.description: Theatre · Samuel Beckett (1953): two tramps wait by a bare tree for someone who never comes — 'a play in which nothing happens, twice', the cornerstone of absurdist theatre, now revived on Broadway.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Emperor of All Maladies – 2026-07-22.html
swipes.title: The Emperor of All Maladies
swipes.description: Siddhartha Mukherjee (2010, Pulitzer): the 5,000-year 'biography' of cancer — from Imhotep and Atossa through Halsted's radical surgery and Farber's first chemotherapy to Nixon's War on Cancer and the targeted-therapy age.

swipes.title: Strukturwandel der Öffentlichkeit
swipes.description: Jürgen Habermas (1962): Wie in Kaffeehäusern und Salons die bürgerliche Öffentlichkeit entstand — und wie kommerzielle Massenmedien, PR und Werbung sie in eine manipulierte Pseudo-Öffentlichkeit zurückverwandeln ('Refeudalisierung').

swipes.file: Swipe – New York Hits Pause on AI – 2026-07-22.html
swipes.title: New York Hits Pause on AI
swipes.description: Odd Lots (Bloomberg, 17 July 2026): Gov. Kathy Hochul on her one-year moratorium on new hyperscale data centers — the first statewide pause in the US, as the AI build-out collides with soaring electricity bills.

swipes.title: Kuba und die USA
swipes.description: Der Rest ist Geschichte (Deutschlandfunk, 16. Juli 2026): Wie aus der Revolution von 1959 das längste Embargo der Welt wurde — von der Schweinebucht über die Kubakrise bis zu Obamas Tauwetter und der neuen Sanktionswelle 2026.

swipes.file: Swipe – Wir kennen die Realität des Krieges – 2026-07-18.html
swipes.title: »Wir kennen die Realität des Krieges«
swipes.description: Das Politikteil (18. Juli 2026): IKRK-Präsidentin Mirjana Spoljaric über Foltergefängnisse, den Geiselaustausch in Gaza und geheime Verhandlungen mit Diktatoren — in einer Welt mit über 130 bewaffneten Konflikten.

swipes.title: Wolfszeit
swipes.description: Harald Jähner (2019, Preis der Leipziger Buchmesse): Deutschland 1945–1955 als Mentalitätsgeschichte — wie aus Volksgenossen über Trümmer, Schwarzmarkt und Beschweigen allmählich wieder Bürger wurden.

swipes.title: Private Truths, Public Lies
swipes.description: Timur Kuran (1995): preference falsification — we misrepresent what we want under social pressure, hated regimes look stable until a minor event triggers the bandwagon, and that's why 1989 surprised everyone.

swipes.title: Machado de Assis
swipes.description: In Our Time (BBC, 11 June 2026): the grandson of freed slaves who became Brazil's greatest writer — his slave-owning readers loved his books, and only later realised he was satirising them. The show's first Brazilian topic.

swipes.file: Swipe – In the Mood for Love – 2026-07-18.html
swipes.title: In the Mood for Love
swipes.description: Film · Wong Kar-wai (2000): shot for 15 months without a screenplay in 1962 Hong Kong, a love story told through restraint — ranked 5th greatest film of all time in the 2022 Sight and Sound poll.

swipes.file: Swipe – You Must Change Your Life – 2026-07-16.html
swipes.title: You Must Change Your Life
swipes.description: Philosophize This! #249 (14 July 2026): Stephen West on Peter Sloterdijk — humans as practicing animals, anthropotechnics, religion reread as training, and Rilke's absolute imperative.

swipes.file: Swipe – Wie Europa Putins Raketenterror stoppen will – 2026-07-16.html
swipes.title: Wie Europa Putins Raketenterror stoppen will
swipes.description: Streitkräfte und Strategien (NDR, 14. Juli 2026): Die Koalition der Willigen schmiedet in Paris ein Raketenabwehr-Bündnis — plus Iran-Eskalation und Thomas Schiller über Europas strategisches Versagen in Afrika.

swipes.title: The Great Leveler
swipes.description: Walter Scheidel (2017): across all of history, only violent shocks — the 'Four Horsemen' of war, revolution, collapse and plague — have seriously reduced inequality. 'Inequality never dies peacefully.'

swipes.title: Lina Bo Bardi
swipes.description: Architecture · Lina Bo Bardi (1914–1992): the Italian-Brazilian who floated MASP above São Paulo on a 74-metre span, hung its paintings on glass easels, and turned a barrel factory into SESC Pompéia.

swipes.title: Geschichte eines Deutschen
swipes.description: Sebastian Haffner (geschrieben 1939, erschienen 2000): Die Erinnerungen 1914–1933 — die Nazifizierung Deutschlands von innen, erzählt als 'Duell' zwischen übermächtigem Staat und Privatmann.

swipes.title: The Prize
swipes.description: Daniel Yergin (1990, Pulitzer 1992): oil created the modern world — from Churchill's navy to Desert Storm, and why 'mastery itself was the prize'.

swipes.title: Krankheit als Lehrmeisterin
swipes.description: WDR 5 Das philosophische Radio (13. Juli 2026): Philosoph Helmut Heit über Nietzsche — für den Gesundheit hieß, Krankheiten produktiv zu nutzen.

swipes.title: How China Remembers
swipes.description: MERICS China Podcast (13 July 2026): historian Rana Mitter on how China's memory of invasion and humiliation shapes its 'New Chinese Nationalism' — and its choices today.

swipes.file: Swipe – Die neue Ordnung auf dem alten Kontinent – 2026-07-15.html
swipes.title: Die neue Ordnung auf dem alten Kontinent
swipes.description: Philipp Ther (2014, Preis der Leipziger Buchmesse): Osteuropa nach 1989 als Labor des Neoliberalismus — Schocktherapie, Boomtowns und die 'Kotransformation' des Westens.

swipes.title: Dancing by Chance
swipes.description: Dance · Merce Cunningham (1919–2009): the choreographer who let coins, dice and the I Ching decide the next step — and rebuilt modern dance around chance.

swipes.title: Yayoi Kusama
swipes.description: Exhibition · Yayoi Kusama at Museum Ludwig, Cologne (until 2 Aug 2026): 300+ works from seven decades — polka dots, happenings and Infinity Mirror Rooms as 'self-obliteration'.

swipes.file: Swipe – Wie wir mit AfD-Waehlern reden – 2026-07-12.html
swipes.title: Wie wir mit AfD-Wähler:innen reden müssen
swipes.description: Lage der Nation LdN486 (8. Juli 2026) mit Psychologin Eva-Lotta Brakemeier: Der Treiber hinter der AfD-Wahl ist oft nicht Ideologie, sondern Abstiegsangst — und man sollte im Gespräch bleiben.

swipes.title: Todesfuge
swipes.description: Lyrik · Paul Celans „Todesfuge“ (1944/45): eine Fuge aus Worten über die Lager — „der Tod ist ein Meister aus Deutschland“ wurde zur Chiffre des Holocaust und 1988 im Bundestag verlesen.

swipes.title: The Waste Land
swipes.description: Poetry · T.S. Eliot's 'The Waste Land' (1922): modernism's central poem — a fragmented, allusive collage that turned the spiritual desolation of post-war Europe into a new kind of verse.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Knots We Tie Ourselves Into – 2026-07-12.html
swipes.title: The Knots We Tie Ourselves Into
swipes.description: Making Sense #483 (1 July 2026): Sam Harris and Alain de Botton on the psychology of unhappiness, the secular world's discomfort with ritual, love as a skill, and the case against meritocracy.

swipes.title: Schuldig — und trotzdem Kandidatin?
swipes.description: Das Politikteil (10. Juli 2026) mit Matthias Krupa: Das Berufungsgericht bestätigt Le Pens „systematische Veruntreuung von EU-Geldern“ — Fußfessel inklusive. Und trotzdem hält sie an der Kandidatur 2027 fest.

swipes.title: Say Nothing
swipes.description: Patrick Radden Keefe (2018): the 1972 disappearance of Jean McConville as a lens on the whole of the Troubles — radicalisation, the Boston College tapes, and what a society owes the disappeared.

swipes.title: Imagined Communities
swipes.description: Benedict Anderson (1983): the nation as an imagined political community — built by print capitalism, mapped by census, map and museum, and loyal enough to die for.

swipes.title: From Choke to Clutch
swipes.description: Freakonomics Radio (3 July 2026): cognitive scientist and Dartmouth president Sian Beilock on why skilled people choke under pressure — worry hijacks working memory — and the counterintuitive fixes.

swipes.title: Empire of Cotton
swipes.description: Sven Beckert (2014): the Industrial Revolution was built on 'war capitalism' — slavery, land expropriation and armed conquest — told through the global history of a single fibre.

swipes.file: Swipe – Die Welt aus den Angeln – 2026-07-12.html
swipes.title: Die Welt aus den Angeln
swipes.description: Philipp Blom (2017): Die Kleine Eiszeit 1570–1700 als Katalysator der Moderne — Aufklärung, Wissenschaft und Kapitalismus als Antworten auf einen Klimasturz, und was das über die heutige Klimakrise lehrt.

swipes.title: Das sowjetische Jahrhundert
swipes.description: Karl Schlögel (2017): keine Systemgeschichte, sondern eine Archäologie des sowjetischen Alltags — Kommunalka, Datscha und Eisenbahn als gelebte Wirklichkeit eines untergegangenen Imperiums.

swipes.file: Swipe – Avoiding War in the South China Sea – 2026-07-12.html
swipes.title: Avoiding War in the South China Sea
swipes.description: Pekingology (CSIS, 9 July 2026): Greg Poling on China's strategy, successes and failures in the South China Sea — ten years to the day after the tribunal voided the nine-dash line.

swipes.file: Swipe – A Radical Vision for Israelis and Palestinians – 2026-07-12.html
swipes.title: A Radical Vision for Israelis and Palestinians
swipes.description: The Ezra Klein Show (7 July 2026): Rula Hardal & May Pundak on 'A Land for All' — two sovereign states sharing one open homeland, with free movement and a shared Jerusalem.

swipes.title: The Platform State
swipes.description: Sinica Podcast (8 July 2026): Angela Huyue Zhang & Alex Yang recast China's state not as a central planner but as a platform company — setting standards and governing an ecosystem of competing private firms.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Father of Modern Drama – 2026-07-10.html
swipes.title: The Father of Modern Drama
swipes.description: Theatre · Henrik Ibsen and 'A Doll's House' (1879): the play that traded melodrama for real life, put a woman's inner world at the centre, and ended on a slammed door — the birth of modern drama.

swipes.title: Roosevelts New Deal
swipes.description: Der Rest ist Geschichte (2. Juli 2026): Franklin D. Roosevelt und der New Deal — wie ein an Polio erkrankter Präsident die Weltwirtschaftskrise mit mehr Staat bekämpfte und als Einziger viermal gewählt wurde.

swipes.title: Gekaufte Zeit
swipes.description: Wolfgang Streeck (2013): Demokratie und Kapitalismus im Dauerstreit — seit den 1970ern wird die fällige Krise mit 'gekaufter Zeit' (Inflation, Schulden) nur vertagt; Staatsvolk gegen Marktvolk.

swipes.title: Die Sezession der Plebejer
swipes.description: Eine Stunde History (3. Juli 2026): 494 v. Chr. verließen die Plebejer Rom und legten die Stadt lahm — der erste 'Streik' der Geschichte erzwang das Amt der Volkstribune.

swipes.title: Die Neuerfindung der Diktatur
swipes.description: Kai Strittmatter (2018): Wie China mit Big Data und KI den perfektesten Überwachungsstaat der Geschichte baut — und damit auch den Westen herausfordert.

swipes.title: Determined
swipes.description: Robert Sapolsky (2023): the neurobiologist's hard case that free will is an illusion — every choice is the sum of biology and environment we never chose.

swipes.title: Betting Against the Crowd
swipes.description: Odd Lots (6 July 2026): the rare 'sharps' who actually make money on Kalshi and Polymarket — by exploiting the systematic biases of everyone else in the crowd.

swipes.title: An Immense World
swipes.description: Ed Yong (2022): every animal lives inside its own sensory bubble — its Umwelt — and what we call reality is only the sliver our own senses can reach.

swipes.title: The Rare Earth Weapon
swipes.description: ChinaTalk (June 2026) with Farrell Gregory & Joris Teer: China refines ~90% of the world's rare earths — how Beijing turned that chokehold into economic coercion, and why subsidies alone can't answer it.

swipes.title: Lincoln und die Union
swipes.description: Der Rest ist Geschichte (25. Juni 2026): Lincolns oberstes Ziel war nicht die Abschaffung der Sklaverei, sondern der Erhalt der Union — für den er mit dem Leben bezahlte.

swipes.title: Forced Rejoicing
swipes.description: Music · Shostakovich's Fifth Symphony (1937): written to survive Stalin, it hands the censor a triumph and the audience its grief — and the finale's 'rejoicing' may be forced.

swipes.file: Swipe – Der lange Weg nach Westen – 2026-07-09.html
swipes.title: Der lange Weg nach Westen
swipes.description: Heinrich August Winkler (2000): deutsche Geschichte 1806–1990 als Sonderweg — eine doppelte Verspätung von Nationalstaat und Demokratie, die erst 1945 und 1990 endete.

swipes.title: Age of Ambition
swipes.description: Evan Osnos (2014): the new China as a collision of two forces — individual aspiration and Party authoritarianism — told through strivers, a blind dissident, and seekers of faith.

swipes.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
swipes.description: Daniel Kahneman (2011, Nobel laureate): the mind runs on two systems — fast, intuitive System 1 and slow, effortful System 2 — and we place far too much confidence in our own judgement.

swipes.title: The Price of Time
swipes.description: Edward Chancellor (2022): interest is the price of time — push rates to zero and finance turns absurd, breeding bubbles, zombie firms and inequality rather than growth.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Origins of Political Order – 2026-07-03.html
swipes.title: The Origins of Political Order
swipes.description: Francis Fukuyama (2011): a working political order needs three things at once — a strong state, the rule of law and accountable government. China built the first modern state; the miracle is balancing all three.

swipes.title: Smiljan Radić
swipes.description: Architecture · Smiljan Radić, the Chilean who won the 2026 Pritzker Prize: buildings that look temporary, unstable or unfinished — resting on raw boulders — yet offer structured, quietly joyful shelter.

swipes.title: Shadows on the Wall
swipes.description: Film · German Expressionist cinema (c.1919–1933): a defeated Weimar Germany painted its nightmares onto film — Caligari, Nosferatu, Metropolis — and invented horror, noir and sci-fi.

swipes.title: Rückzug aus Europa
swipes.description: Streitkräfte und Strategien (NDR, Juni 2026): Die USA prüfen einen beschleunigten Truppenabzug aus Europa — und Europa muss die Lücke im NATO-Streitkräftemodell selbst füllen.

swipes.title: Müdigkeitsgesellschaft
swipes.description: Byung-Chul Han (2010): Wir leben nicht mehr in Foucaults Disziplinar-, sondern in einer Leistungsgesellschaft — und beuten uns freiwillig selbst aus, bis zum Burnout.

swipes.title: Der „Mafia-Staat“
swipes.description: Lage der Nation LdN485 (1. Juli 2026) mit Gesine Dornblüth: Russland jenseits der Klischees — warum die Demokratie scheiterte, Gewalt zum Mittel der Politik wurde und ein Kriegsende auch Putins Ende wäre.

swipes.title: China's Growth Machine
swipes.description: Ones and Tooze from 'Summer Davos' in Dalian (June 2026): Adam Tooze on Chinese 'overcapacity' as a global gift, the 15th Five-Year Plan, and a country running two economies at once.

swipes.title: 1913. Der Sommer des Jahrhunderts
swipes.description: Florian Illies (2012): eine kulturelle Chronik des Jahres vor der Katastrophe, Monat für Monat — als Kafka, Freud, Strawinsky und die kommenden Diktatoren gleichzeitig auf der Bühne standen.

swipes.title: Welt in Aufruhr
swipes.description: Herfried Münkler (2023): Die liberale Weltordnung ist an ihre Grenzen gekommen; wahrscheinlichstes Zukunftsmodell ist eine Pentarchie aus USA, China, Russland, Indien und EU.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Making of the Atomic Bomb – 2026-07-02.html
swipes.title: The Making of the Atomic Bomb
swipes.description: Richard Rhodes (1986, Pulitzer): part biography of the scientists, part biography of the atom itself — from Szilárd's chain-reaction insight at a London stoplight to Trinity and the moral reckoning.

swipes.title: Spain's China Gambit
swipes.description: Sinica Podcast (22 April 2026) with Mario Esteban Rodríguez: is Pedro Sánchez's turn to Beijing a shrewd bid for European strategic autonomy — or an increasingly one-sided pilgrimage?

swipes.file: Swipe – Der Mond und die Menschen – 2026-07-02.html
swipes.title: Der Mond und die Menschen
swipes.description: Der Rest ist Geschichte (Deutschlandfunk, 21. Mai 2026): Die uralte Mond-Sehnsucht trifft auf einen neuen Wettlauf — USA (Landung 2028) und China (2030) verfolgen fast identische Pläne.

swipes.title: Café Müller
swipes.description: Dance · Pina Bausch's 'Café Müller' (1978): dancers stumble through a deserted café with eyes closed — Tanztheater that dissolves the line between dance and drama, choreographed from memory.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Risks America Poses to the World – 2026-06-28.html
swipes.title: The Risks America Poses to the World
swipes.description: The Ezra Klein Show (2 June 2026): Ian Bremmer on the Iran war and the Trump–Xi summit, and why US foreign policy has entered 'a period of absolute incoherence' — with America now a source of global risk.

swipes.title: The Box
swipes.description: Marc Levinson: globalisation was built not by trade treaties but by a plain steel shipping container, which slashed transport costs and let factories move anywhere — making Asia the world's workshop.

swipes.title: Kritik der ökonomischen Vernunft
swipes.description: WDR 5 Das philosophische Radio (8. Juni 2026): Der Philosoph Carsten Lotz darüber, wie das Nutzenkalkül des Homo oeconomicus alle Lebensbereiche durchdringt — und warum es zurückgedrängt gehört.

swipes.title: Du Fu
swipes.description: Poetry · Du Fu (712–770), the Tang 'poet-sage' ranked with Li Bai as China's greatest poet: from 'Spring View' to his ruined Chengdu cottage, he turned an empire's collapse into the most human verse.

swipes.title: Die Gesellschaft der Singularitäten
swipes.description: Andreas Reckwitz: Die Spätmoderne folgt einer sozialen Logik des Besonderen — das Einzigartige wird prämiert, das Allgemeine entwertet. Kehrseite der Singularisierung: eine neue Klassengesellschaft.

swipes.title: Washington vs. Pontiac
swipes.description: ZEIT Geschichte (30. Mai 2026): Wie die USA auch aus dem Kampf um Land entstanden — vom 16-jährigen Landvermesser Washington bis zur Allianz des Ottawa-Anführers Pontiac. Land, das nie leer war.

swipes.title: WarTalk: The BS Détente
swipes.description: ChinaTalk (25 June 2026): Ely Ratner on why a hollow détente with Beijing leaves US allies hedging, whether Taiwan is the better dollar-for-dollar defence buy, and the Iran-war shadow over American resolve.

swipes.title: The Ambassadors
swipes.description: Hans Holbein the Younger (1533, National Gallery): two French envoys, a shelf of human knowledge — and an anamorphic skull that only resolves when you view the picture from the side.

swipes.title: The Alignment Problem
swipes.description: Brian Christian: making AI capture what we actually mean — from a biased courtroom algorithm (COMPAS) to existential risk — is two sides of one coin, and computing's most urgent question.

swipes.title: Resonanz
swipes.description: Hartmut Rosa: Lebensqualität misst sich nicht an Ressourcen, sondern an unserer Weltbeziehung. Wenn Beschleunigung das Problem ist, dann ist Resonanz vielleicht die Lösung.

swipes.file: Swipe – Paul Kennedy on Great Powers – 2026-06-27.html
swipes.title: Paul Kennedy on Great Powers
swipes.description: ChinaTalk (10 June 2026): historian Paul Kennedy on imperial overstretch, 'sensible retrenchment', and why in a tripolar US–China–India world, 'shipbuilding is real'.

swipes.title: Las Meninas
swipes.description: Diego Velázquez (1656, Museo del Prado): a court portrait that became a painting about painting — layered gazes, a mirror placing the king and queen where you stand, and the viewer pulled into the frame.

swipes.title: Joseph Roth
swipes.description: In Our Time (BBC, 4 June 2026): the stateless novelist of 'The Radetzky March' who mourned the Habsburg empire and foresaw the new nationalism that burned his books.

swipes.file: Swipe – How the World Really Works – 2026-06-27.html
swipes.title: How the World Really Works
swipes.description: Vaclav Smil: modern civilisation rests on four material pillars — ammonia, steel, concrete, plastics — and on the fossil fuels we can't yet replace. We don't eat sunlight; we partly eat oil.

swipes.file: Swipe – How Chinese AI Is Different – 2026-06-27.html
swipes.title: How Chinese AI Is Different
swipes.description: Odd Lots (15 June 2026): Grace Shao on how China's AI sector adapted to chip controls — cheap frontier models since DeepSeek, conditional Nvidia H200 access, and giants like Baidu going full-stack.

swipes.title: Episches Theater
swipes.description: Theater · Bertolt Brecht und der Verfremdungseffekt: ein 'Theater des wissenschaftlichen Zeitalters', das kritisches Mitdenken statt Einfühlung will — und trotzdem amüsiert.

swipes.title: Die Verwandlung der Welt
swipes.description: Jürgen Osterhammel erzählt das 19. Jahrhundert global statt eurozentrisch: wie Westeuropa zum Modell wurde, die Macht sich ungleich verschob — Sklaverei, Imperien, Industrialisierung in einer vernetzten Welt.

swipes.title: Die Höhle von Chauvet
swipes.description: Eine Stunde History (Deutschlandfunk Nova): Auftakt der neuen 100-teiligen Chronik — die rund 36.000 Jahre alten Höhlenmalereien von Chauvet, 1994 im Ardèche-Tal entdeckt.

swipes.file: Swipe – Der Wanderer über dem Nebelmeer – 2026-06-27.html
swipes.title: Der Wanderer über dem Nebelmeer
swipes.description: Caspar David Friedrich (um 1818, Hamburger Kunsthalle): die berühmteste Rückenfigur der Kunst. Wir sehen nie sein Gesicht, nur das Nebelmeer — Sinnbild des romantischen Erhabenen.

swipes.title: Debt: The First 5,000 Years
swipes.description: David Graeber: the textbook story of barter → money → credit is backwards. Credit came first, money began as 'social currency', and debt is the oldest economic technology we have.

swipes.title: Das Ende von FCAS
swipes.description: Streitkräfte und Strategien (NDR, 5. Juni 2026): Europas größtes Rüstungsprojekt FCAS ist am Streit zwischen Dassault und Airbus über Führung, Arbeitsanteile und geistiges Eigentum zerbrochen.

swipes.title: A 60-Day Window
swipes.description: Iran: The Latest (The Telegraph, late June 2026): inside the fragile US–Iran ceasefire framework, the fight over the Strait of Hormuz and the 60-day nuclear clock.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Garden of Earthly Delights – 2026-06-25.html
swipes.title: The Garden of Earthly Delights
swipes.description: Hieronymus Bosch (c. 1490–1500, Prado): a four-metre triptych running Eden → a teeming pleasure garden → a nightmarish Hell — and 500 years of unsettled argument over what it means.

swipes.title: The Dictator's Handbook
swipes.description: Bueno de Mesquita & Smith — selectorate theory: every leader survives by paying off the small group of 'essentials' whose support is vital. Why bad behaviour is almost always good politics.

swipes.title: Seeing Like a State
swipes.description: James C. Scott — why grand, 'scientific' schemes to improve society (planned cities, rational forests) keep failing: the state's drive for legibility erases the local knowledge (mētis) that real societies run on.

swipes.title: Postwar
swipes.description: Tony Judt's history of Europe since 1945: the continent rebuilt itself by forgetting the war, then defined itself by remembering it — East and West told as one story.

swipes.title: Melencolia I
swipes.description: Albrecht Dürer (1514): a winged genius sits idle amid the tools of knowledge — a magic square, a baffling polyhedron, an hourglass. Art's most enigmatic meditation on creativity and its limits.

swipes.title: Kingdom of Fossil Water
swipes.description: In Our Time (BBC, 11 June 2026) on the Garamantes — a Saharan civilisation that mined ancient 'fossil water' through a thousand miles of tunnels, built desert cities, then drained the aquifer that fed it.

swipes.title: How Asia Works
swipes.description: Joe Studwell: why Japan, Korea, Taiwan and China got rich and their neighbours didn't — a three-step recipe of land reform, export discipline and financial repression, in that order.

swipes.title: Die Reformen des Kleisthenes
swipes.description: Eine Stunde History (Deutschlandfunk Nova): 508 v. Chr. ordnete Kleisthenes die Bürgerschaft Athens nach Wohnort statt Abstammung neu — zehn Phylen, der Rat der 500 und das Fundament der Demokratie.

swipes.title: Dancing in Shackles
swipes.description: Sinica with Yi-Ling Liu on 'The Wall Dancers': China's internet told through five lives — rapper, censor, app founder, feminist, sci-fi writer — and the end of techno-utopianism.

swipes.title: Why Nations Fail
swipes.description: Acemoglu & Robinson (2024 Nobel): two towns share a name and a fence — one rich, one poor. The difference isn't geography or culture; it's inclusive vs extractive institutions.

swipes.title: The Grid's Hidden Chokepoint
swipes.description: ChinaTalk (20 June 2026): even an infinite supply of transformers wouldn't fix the grid. The deeper bottleneck is power electronics — and it runs through gallium, which China controls ~99% of.

swipes.title: The Great Wave
swipes.description: Hokusai's 'Great Wave off Kanagawa' (c.1831): not a tsunami but a Buddhist image of impermanence, printed in a brand-new blue accidentally invented in Berlin.

swipes.title: The Avoidable War
swipes.description: Kevin Rudd on US–China: war isn't driven by what each side wants, but by how badly each misreads the other — and 'managed strategic competition' as the way out.

swipes.title: Sapiens
swipes.description: Yuval Noah Harari: we run the planet not because we're strong or clever, but because we alone can believe in shared fictions — money, nations, gods — that let millions of strangers cooperate.

swipes.file: Swipe – Running a Bank in the Age of AI – 2026-06-24.html
swipes.title: Running a Bank in the Age of AI
swipes.description: Odd Lots with Goldman's David Solomon (4 June 2026): AI can draft 95% of an IPO filing in minutes — yet he bets there's no white-collar wipeout coming.

swipes.title: Material World
swipes.description: Ed Conway on the six raw materials — sand, salt, iron, copper, oil, lithium — that still rule a supposedly weightless digital age. We've never dug up more stuff.

swipes.title: Die Macht der Geografie
swipes.description: Das Politikteil (DIE ZEIT, 12. Juni 2026): Eine Meerenge von kaum 33 Kilometern – die Straße von Hormus – entscheidet über rund ein Fünftel des Welt-Öls. Wie Lage zu Macht wird.

swipes.title: Der Drohnenkrieg
swipes.description: Der Rest ist Geschichte (Deutschlandfunk, 4. Juni 2026): Eine Drohne für ein paar hundert Dollar zerstört einen Panzer für Millionen – wie billige Drohnen die Kriegsführung neu ordnen.

swipes.file: Swipe – A City on a Scroll – 2026-06-24.html
swipes.title: A City on a Scroll
swipes.description: Zhang Zeduan's 'Along the River During the Qingming Festival' (12th c.): a five-metre handscroll that packs 814 people into one continuous panorama of the Song capital — a census of everyday life in ink.

swipes.file: Swipe – Wer bin ich und wenn ja, wie viele – 2026-06-23.html
swipes.title: Wer bin ich – und wenn ja, wie viele?
swipes.description: Richard David Precht — eine philosophische Reise entlang Kants drei Fragen, die große Philosophie mit Hirnforschung verbindet.

swipes.title: Warcraft & Fartcraft
swipes.description: MacroVoices #535 — Michael Every (Rabobank) and Rory Johnston on Iran, inflation, China and the commodity outlook.

swipes.title: The Coming Wave
swipes.description: Mustafa Suleyman with Michael Bhaskar — The Coming Wave: the DeepMind co-founder argues the AI-and-synthetic-biology wave may be uncontainable, the century's defining dilemma.

swipes.title: The Comedian Returns
swipes.description: Exhibition · 'Guggenheim Pop: 1960 to Now' (New York, 5 Jun 2026 – 10 Jan 2027): 29 artists, Warhol and Lichtenstein beside Cattelan's banana, and Oldenburg's Soft Shuttlecock back after 25 years.

swipes.title: Sublime Poetry
swipes.description: Raphael: Sublime Poetry at the Met — the first comprehensive US Raphael show, 170+ masterpieces, closing 28 June 2026.

swipes.title: Lügenfritz
swipes.description: Lage der Nation LdN483 (17. Juni 2026): Ein Facebook-Nutzer wurde für die Bezeichnung von Kanzler Merz als „Lügenfritze“ verurteilt — wie weit reicht die Meinungsfreiheit gegenüber Politikern?

swipes.title: Die Thukydides-Falle
swipes.description: Sicherheitshalber Folge 109 — führen Machtverschiebungen wirklich zwangsläufig zum Krieg? Plus eine FCAS-Autopsie.

swipes.title: Chip War
swipes.description: Chris Miller — Chip War: semiconductors are the new oil, and the world's most critical supply runs through one contested island, Taiwan.

swipes.title: A Story-Telling Animal
swipes.description: Philosophize This! on Alasdair MacIntyre — no one is self-made; you can only act once you know what story you're a part of.

swipes.file: Swipe – Warum es die Welt nicht gibt – 2026-06-22.html
swipes.title: Warum es die Welt nicht gibt
swipes.description: Daily swipe für Simon. Heute: Markus Gabriel — 'Warum es die Welt nicht gibt' (2013). Der Neue Realismus in einem Satz: Es gibt unzählige Sinnfelder, in denen Dinge erscheinen — nur das All-Umfassende, die Welt, gibt es nicht. Aber alles andere schon.

swipes.title: The Wood Wide Web
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Merlin Sheldrake — 'Entangled Life: How Fungi Make Our Worlds, Change Our Minds & Shape Our Futures' (2020). More than 90% of plants depend on fungi they cannot see — and once you take fungi seriously, the line between one individual and another starts to dissolve: 'We are all lichens.'

swipes.title: The Game of Doubt
swipes.description: Freakonomics Radio — an ancient dice game, backgammon, as a school for thinking under uncertainty.

swipes.title: The Code of Life
swipes.description: Walter Isaacson — The Code Breaker: a defence system stolen from bacteria became a tool to rewrite our own species.

swipes.file: Swipe – I Am Not a Designer – 2026-06-22.html
swipes.title: I Am Not a Designer
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Isamu Noguchi (1904–1988), ahead of the High Museum's design retrospective 'I am not a designer' (Atlanta 2026 → Peabody Essex Museum). Brâncuși's assistant, Martha Graham's set designer, maker of the Akari lamp — a six-decade career that refused every label.

swipes.title: Europäische Katastrophe
swipes.description: Daily swipe für Simon. Heute: Herfried Münkler — 'Der Dreißigjährige Krieg. Europäische Katastrophe, deutsches Trauma 1618–1648'. Ein 975-Seiten-Standardwerk — und Münklers These: kein späterer Konflikt erklärt die Kriege der Gegenwart besser als dieser.

swipes.title: Brexit, Ten Years On
swipes.description: Ones and Tooze — a decade after the vote, the economic bill is still arriving, quietly, every year.

swipes.title: All Under Heaven
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Sinica — 'To Rule All Under Heaven: Andrew Seth Meyer on the Warring States' (Kaiser Kuo, 21 May 2026). The 260 years between Confucius and the First Emperor wrote the operating system Chinese political life still runs on — and why reaching for Han Feizi to decode Xi is mostly lazy essentialism.

swipes.title: A Second Life
swipes.description: Tracey Emin — A Second Life at Tate Modern: a 40-year retrospective, from the bad girl of Britart to one of its great painters of mortality.

swipes.title: The Survivors
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Charles C. Mann — '1491: New Revelations of the Americas Before Columbus' (Knopf, 2005; National Academies Communication Award 2006). The Americas of 1491 may have held more people than Europe — and what Europeans called wilderness was a depopulated garden.

swipes.file: Swipe – The Making of an Icon – 2026-06-21.html
swipes.title: The Making of an Icon
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Frida Kahlo, ahead of Tate Modern's 'Frida: The Making of an Icon' (25 June 2026 – 3 January 2027). How a chronically ill painter from a blue house in Coyoacán became the most reproduced female artist on Earth — and what she lost in the translation.

swipes.title: Power and Progress
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Daron Acemoglu & Simon Johnson — 'Power and Progress: Our Thousand-Year Struggle Over Technology and Prosperity' (Public Affairs, 2023). The book that won the 2024 Nobel team a global hearing: technology has no preordained direction, and shared prosperity is a political choice.

swipes.title: Into the Void
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Anish Kapoor at the Hayward Gallery, London (16 June – 18 October 2026). Curated by Ralph Rugoff for his final show as director, the artist's first major UK survey at the Hayward in nearly 30 years — Vantablack voids, mirror sculptures, and a six-metre inflatable that swallows the room.

swipes.title: How Progress Ends
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Mark Leonard's World in 30 Minutes — 'How progress ends' with Carl Benedikt Frey (ECFR, June 2026). Computing, the internet, AI — and productivity is still stagnating. The Oxford economist on why stagnation, not innovation, is the historical default.

swipes.title: Carving the Essence
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Constantin Brâncuși at the Neue Nationalgalerie, Berlin (20 March – 9 August 2026, in cooperation with Centre Pompidou). The first comprehensive German Brâncuși show in over 50 years — 150+ works, plus a partial reconstruction of the Paris studio. The man who taught modern sculpture to subtract.

swipes.title: The Permabear Returns
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Odd Lots — 'Jeremy Grantham on How to Tell If a Bubble Is About to Burst' (Bloomberg, 18 June 2026). The 87-year-old who called 1989 Japan, 2000 dot-com, and 2008 housing says he sees the pattern again — and this time it has a name: AI.

swipes.title: The Dinner-Plate Chip
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Odd Lots with Andrew Feldman — 'Why Cerebras CEO Andrew Feldman Built The World's Largest Computer Chip' (21 May 2026). A dinner-plate-sized chip, a $20B OpenAI contract, and a $56B IPO bet against Nvidia's GPU orthodoxy.

swipes.title: The Lost Virtue
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: The Ezra Klein Show with Helena Rosenblatt — 'The Book That Changed How I Think About Liberalism' (5 May 2026). Liberalism began as a virtue, not a political philosophy. The history was lost. That's why the defence feels so arid.

swipes.title: Born in Bohemia
swipes.description: Daily swipe for Simon. Today: Odd Lots — Brendan Greeley on the 500-year history of the dollar. The dollar is older than the United States — and never really belonged to it.
